divergences.decision: MM concurrency and memory-safety constraint: shared mutable state must have a safe ownership/visibility model, and hot-path changes must preserve correctness before availability/performance (arc42 section 2.2; prd section 3; QUESTION_TREE Q3.2.2/Q4.1; repository engineering principles).
divergences.detail: The new UrmaSuccessRateTracker is shared request-path state updated by concurrent Set/Get result recording. During window rollover, RecordUrmaResult first publishes the new window start with compare_exchange_strong, then exchanges the counters. A concurrent recorder can observe the new start and increment the new window before the rollover thread exchanges the counters, so that new-window sample can be consumed into the expired-window snapshot or cleared. This makes the tracker state non-linearizable and can undercount or misclassify later windows, which diverges from the MM's concurrency-safety requirement for shared mutable state in infrastructure hot paths.
divergences.evidence: src/datasystem/client/urma_success_rate_tracker.cpp:hunk adding RecordUrmaResult; src/datasystem/client/client_worker_common_api.cpp:hunk adding ClientWorkerRemoteCommonApi::RecordUrmaDataPlaneResult; src/datasystem/client/object_cache/client_worker_api/client_worker_base_api.cpp:hunk recording URMA results on Set/Publish; src/datasystem/client/object_cache/client_worker_api/client_worker_remote_api.cpp:hunk recording Get-side URMA results
divergences.fix: Make window rollover atomic with respect to sample recording, for example by protecting the rollover/counter handoff with a small mutex or by using per-window generation/counter buckets so recorders cannot write into counters that a rollover thread is about to exchange. Keep the request-path critical section minimal and retain the existing UT/ST coverage, adding a concurrent rollover test that verifies post-rollover samples remain in the new window.
